name: Indonesia Prehistory and Protohistory (draft v1)
description: Draft chronology focused on Indonesian prehistory and protohistory. Built from user-supplied screenshots and the locally extracted PDF corpus for ArkeOpen personalization.
sources: User screenshots in this chat | Local PDF extraction corpus in data/google-drive-text

name: Geological Epochs
description: Grouping node for geological epochs used in the chronology menu.

name: Pleistocene
description: The Pleistocene spans from 2.58 mya to 11.7 kya.
sources: Screenshot: Geological epochs timeline showing Pleistocene from ~2.5 mya to ~11.7 kya

name: Early Pleistocene
description: Early Pleistocene from 2.5 mya to ~498 kya.
sources: Screenshot: Geological epochs timeline showing ~0.5 mya marker

name: Middle Pleistocene
description: Middle Pleistocene from ~498 kya to ~127 kya.
sources: Screenshot: Geological epochs timeline showing ~0.5 mya and ~129 kya markers

name: Late Pleistocene
description: Late Pleistocene from ~127 kya to 11.7 kya.
sources: Screenshot: Geological epochs timeline showing Late Pleistocene starting at ~129 kya

name: Holocene
description: The Holocene begins at 11.7 kya and continues to the present.
sources: Screenshot: “The Holocene has spanned the past 11,700 years” | Timeline screenshot with Early/Mid/Late Holocene

name: Early Holocene
description: Early Holocene from 11.7 kya to 8.2 kya.
sources: Timeline screenshot with Early Holocene ending at ~8.2 kya

name: Mid Holocene
description: Mid Holocene from 8.2 kya to 4.2 kya.
sources: Timeline screenshot with Mid Holocene ending at ~4.2 kya

name: Late Holocene
sources: Timeline screenshot with Late Holocene after ~4.2 kya

name: Archaeological Periods
description: Grouping node for archaeological periods used in the chronology menu.

name: Palaeolithic
description: Broad Indonesian Palaeolithic draft spanning the Pleistocene record of stone tools and archaic hominins through the terminal Pleistocene.
sources: Timeline screenshot: appearance of stone tools in Java and Flores during the Pleistocene | Screenshot of Lower/Middle/Upper Pleistocene archaeological industries

name: Neolithic
description: Draft Indonesian Neolithic based on a general Island Southeast Asia range of c. 4000 to 2300/2100 BP, with overlap into the earliest metal-bearing contexts.
sources: Screenshot: “The Neolithic in ISEA ranges from c. 4000–2300/2100 BP” | Screenshot: Indonesian Neolithic traced to Austronesian-speaking people entering at ca. 4000 BP

name: Early Metal Age (Paleometallic)
description: Draft Early Metal Age based on a regional onset between 2500 and 2000 cal BP and extending until the beginning of the early historical/protohistoric horizon after 400 CE.
sources: Screenshot: “Between 2500 and 2000 cal BP … the Early Metal Phase began” | Screenshot: “Early Metal Age … items of copper/bronze dating to about 600–500 BCE … The period after 400 CE is essentially Early Historical”

name: Protohistory (Early Historical Period)
description: Draft protohistoric horizon beginning after 400 CE. The upper bound at 1500 CE is a temporary local cutoff for ArkeOpen filtering and should be revised against your regional literature review.
sources: Screenshot: “PROTOHISTORY (EARLY HISTORICAL PERIOD) AFTER 400 CE” | Derived local cutoff for draft implementation
